How Widget Calibration Works

By Jane Smith — Principal Calibration Engineer. Reviewed by Omar Haddad.

Calibration is the process of aligning a widget's measured output with a known reference standard. In production environments, drift accumulates from thermal cycling and mechanical wear, so calibration intervals matter as much as the initial alignment.

Why calibration drifts

Thermal expansion changes sensor geometry by measurable microns.

Recommended intervals

Most production lines re-calibrate every 90 days or 10,000 cycles.
